text: The ZNIX Framework shall provide an object-based information management system.
The ZNIX Framework shall expose lower level functionality through a public API.
The ZNIX Framework shall include a proxy mechanism to enable ZNIX-unaware applications to utilize Framework capabilities.
The ZNIX Framework shall support systems with 256 megabytes of RAM.
The ZNIX Framework shall provide a centralized archive for user information.
The ZNIX Framework shall provide a hierarchical model for managing archived information.
The ZNIX Framework shall allow the user to add arbitrary pieces of information without disrupting the hierarchy.
The ZNIX Framework shall allow the user to remove arbitrary pieces of information without disrupting the hierarchy.
The ZNIX Framework shall allow the user to modify arbitrary pieces of information without disrupting the hierarchy.
The ZNIX Framework shall allow the user to back up arbitrary pieces of information to removable storage devices without disrupting the hierarchy.
The ZNIX Framework shall automate data capturing tasks to assist the user in building information hierarchies.
The ZNIX Framework shall automate data categorizing tasks to assist the user in building information hierarchies.
The browser GUI shall be designed for manipulation by casual users with basic IT skills.
The system design must be compatible with Intel Pentium III or compatible 1-GHz PC hardware.
The Framework shall capture information from Outlook Express version 6.0.
The Framework shall capture information from Outlook 2003 version 11.5.
The Framework shall capture information from Address Book version 6.0.
The Framework shall capture information from Internet Explorer version 6.0.
The Framework shall capture information from Opera version 7.5.
The Framework shall capture information from Firefox version 1.0.
The Framework shall capture information from Word 2003 version 11.5.
The Framework shall intercept outbound email before it is dispatched to the SMTP server.
The Framework shall fetch inbound email from the POP3 server.
The Framework shall create an Associate to contain the email message body.
The Framework shall link created Associates to corresponding People Entities.
The Framework shall permanently store web sites visited during a user-specified research project.
The Framework shall provide a GUI that allows users to create relationships between textual documents and Entities.
The Framework shall automatically save documents to disk in a manner reflecting their relationships to Entities.
The Framework shall include a browser capable of displaying the information hierarchy in a UI similar to Windows Explorer.
The Framework shall leave residue on the data store to indicate when a portion of data has been archived to removable media.
The public API shall provide functionality to create Entities and Entity groups.
The public API shall provide functionality to delete Entities and Entity groups.
The public API shall provide functionality to modify Entities and Entity groups.
The public API shall provide functionality to list and access Entities.
The public API shall provide functionality to execute queries for the retrieval of Entities.
The mean time between failures (MTBF) shall exceed 3 months.
The Framework Shell should not utilize more than 40 megabytes of RAM.
The Framework must guard against malicious scripts on remote websites accessing its information.
The Framework must guard against unintended corruption of information by the user.
The system shall conform to ISO 10646 (Unicode UTF-8) standards for character set encoding.
